docs.title: Welcome to Apaleo API Client
docs.text: A powerful, async-first Python client library for the Apaleo API, providing seamless integration with Apaleo's comprehensive hospitality and travel management platform.

docs.title: What is Apaleo API Client?
docs.text:
The Apaleo API Client is a production-ready Python library that wraps the Apaleo Swagger API, offering both synchronous and asynchronous access to Apaleo's features and functionalities. Built with modern Python best practices, it provides:

  • 🔐 OAuth2 Authentication - Automatic token management with refresh capabilities
  • ⚡ Async/Await Support - Built for high-performance concurrent operations
  • 📝 Type Safety - Comprehensive Pydantic models for all requests and responses
  • 🛡️ Error Handling - Domain-specific exceptions with detailed error information
  • 📄 Pagination - Smart concurrent batch fetching for large datasets
  • 🔧 Extensible - Modular adapter pattern supporting all Apaleo API domains

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Get started with Apaleo API Client in just a few lines:

from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient

# Initialize the client
client = ApaleoAPIClient(
    client_id="your-client-id",
    client_secret="your-client-secret"
)

# Fetch properties asynchronously
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties()
print(f"Found {properties.count} properties")

# Clean up
await client.aclose()

docs.title: Comprehensive API Coverage
docs.text:
Supports all major Apaleo API domains:

  • Core API: Properties, inventory, rates, and units management
  • Identity API: OAuth2 authentication and user management
  • Payment API: Payment processing and transaction handling
  • Webhook API: Event-driven integrations
  • Integration API: Third-party system connections
  • Fiscalization API: Tax and compliance management
  • Profile API: User profile and preference management

docs.title: Developer Experience
docs.text:
• Type Complete: Full type hints and IDE autocompletion
  • Async Native: Built on httpx for optimal performance
  • Testing Ready: Comprehensive test coverage and dry-run mode
  • Well Documented: Detailed API reference and examples

docs.title: Production Ready
docs.text:
• Robust Error Handling: Typed exceptions for all HTTP status codes
  • Automatic Retries: Configurable retry logic for transient failures
  • Rate Limiting: Built-in concurrent request management
  • Logging Integration: Comprehensive logging for debugging and monitoring

docs.title: Supported APIs
docs.text:
The client provides access to these Apaleo API endpoints:

API Domain Base URL Version Status Coverage Core https://api.apaleo.com/ V1 🔧 In Progress 1% Identity https://identity.apaleo.com/ V1 ✅ Implemented 100% Payment https://payment.apaleo.com/ V1 🔧 In Progress 0% Webhook https://webhook.apaleo.com/ V1 🔧 In Progress 0% Integration https://integration.apaleo.com/ V1 📋 Planned 0% Fiscalization https://fiscalization.apaleo.com/ V1 📋 Planned 0% Profile https://profile.apaleo.com/ V1 📋 Planned 0%

docs.title: Core API
docs.text:
This section covers the Core API endpoints, which provide access to Apaleo's core functionalities such as properties, inventory, rates, and units management. The Core API is the foundation of the Apaleo ecosystem and is essential for most integrations.

It will be automatically generated from docstrings and type hints in the codebase. For usage examples and guides, see the User Guide and Why Choose Apaleo API Client pages.

docs.title: Client Configuration and Usage
docs.text: The Apaleo API Client is designed to be flexible and easy to use. This section provides an overview of how to configure and use the client effectively in your applications.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to the Apaleo API Client will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Version Policy
docs.text:
We follow Semantic Versioning:

  • MAJOR (X.0.0): Breaking changes to public APIs
  • MINOR (0.X.0): New features, backwards compatible
  • PATCH (0.0.X): Bug fixes, backwards compatible

View releases on GitHub

docs.title: Contributing to Apaleo API Client
docs.text: We welcome contributions to the Apaleo API Client! Whether you're fixing bugs, adding features, improving documentation, or helping with testing, your contributions are valued.

docs.title: 1. Set Up Development Environment
docs.text:
# Fork the repository on GitHub first, then clone
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/apaleo-api-client.git
cd apaleo-api-client

# Install with all development dependencies
poetry install --with dev,test,lint,docs

# Activate the virtual environment
poetry shell

# Verify installation
inv help

docs.title: 2. Check Everything Works
docs.text:
# Run tests
inv test

# Run linting
inv lint

# Format code
inv format

# Build documentation
inv docs

docs.title: 3. Make Your Changes
docs.text:
# Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

# Make your changes
# ...

# Run tests and linting
inv test
inv lint

# Commit your changes
git add .
git commit -m "feat: add your feature description"

# Push to your fork
git push origin feature/your-feature-name

docs.title: 4. Submit a Pull Request
docs.text:
1. Go to the GitHub repository
  2. Click "New Pull Request"
  3. Select your branch
  4. Fill out the PR template
  5. Wait for review feedback

docs.title: 📄 Project Structure
docs.text:
Understanding the codebase structure:

src/apaleoapi/
├── __init__.py                # Package exports
├── client.py                  # Main ApaleoAPIClient
├── constants.py               # API endpoints and constants
├── exceptions.py              # Custom exception classes
├── logging.py                 # Logging configuration
├── schemas.py                 # Pydantic schema definitions
├── typing.py                  # Type definitions
│
├── apaleo/                    # API domain modules
│   ├── core/                  # Core API (properties, inventory)
│   ├── identity/              # Identity & authentication
│   ├── payment/               # Payment processing
│   ├── webhook/               # Webhook management
│   ├── integration/           # Third-party integrations
│   ├── fiscalization/         # Tax and compliance
│   └── profile/               # User profiles
│
├── http/                      # HTTP layer
│   ├── auth.py                # OAuth2 authentication
│   └── transport.py           # HTTP transport layer
│
└── services/                  # Support services
    ├── list_fetcher.py        # Pagination handling
    ├── response_handler.py    # Response processing
    └── response_validator.py  # Response validation

docs.title: Available Tasks (via invoke)
docs.text:
inv help                 # Show all available tasks

# Development
inv test                 # Run tests with pytest
inv test-nox             # Test across Python 3.10-3.13

# Code Quality
inv lint                 # Run all linters (ruff, mypy, etc.)
inv format               # Format code (ruff format, isort)
inv check                # Check code quality without fixing

# Documentation  
inv docs                 # Build and serve documentation
inv docs-build           # Build documentation only
inv docs-clean           # Clean generated docs

# Building
inv build                # Build package
inv clean                # Clean build artifacts

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
We use these tools for code quality:

  • ruff: Fast Python linter and formatter
  • mypy: Static type checking
  • isort: Import sorting
  • pytest: Testing framework

docs.title: Code Style Rules
docs.text:
• Line length: 100 characters
  • Use type hints for all public APIs
  • Follow PEP 8 and Black formatting
  • Docstrings for all public functions/classes
  • Use dataclasses for structured data

docs.title: 📅 Release Process
docs.text: (For maintainers)

docs.title: Version Bumping
docs.text:
We use semantic versioning:

  • MAJOR: Breaking API changes
  • MINOR: New features, backwards compatible
  • PATCH: Bug fixes, backwards compatible
# Update version in pyproject.toml
poetry version patch|minor|major

# Update CHANGELOG.md
# Git commit and tag
git add .
git commit -m "chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z"
git tag vX.Y.Z
git push origin main --tags

docs.title: Release Checklist
docs.text:
• All tests pass across Python 3.10-3.13
  • Documentation is up to date
  • CHANGELOG.md is updated
  • Version is bumped in pyproject.toml
  • Git tag matches package version
  • GitHub release is created
  • Package is published to PyPI

docs.title: Performance Contributions
docs.text:
• Use asyncio profiling for async code
  • Benchmark before/after changes
  • Consider memory usage for large datasets
  • Test with various network conditions

docs.title: Security Contributions
docs.text:
• Never log sensitive information (credentials, tokens)
  • Use environment variables for test credentials
  • Follow OWASP guidelines for HTTP clients
  • Consider timing attacks in authentication

docs.title: API Design Principles
docs.text:
• Favor async methods for I/O operations
  • Use type hints for all public APIs
  • Make required parameters explicit
  • Provide sensible defaults for optional parameters
  • Use dataclasses for complex parameters
  • Handle errors gracefully with typed exceptions

docs.title: 📉 Commit Message Format
docs.text:
We follow Conventional Commits:

<type>([scope]): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]

Types: - feat: A new feature - fix: A bug fix - docs: Documentation changes - style: Code style changes (formatting, etc.) - refactor: Code changes that neither fix bugs nor add features - test: Adding or updating tests - chore: Maintenance tasks

Examples: 

feat(core): add property search functionality

fix(auth): handle token refresh race condition

docs: update installation guide for Poetry users

test(integration): add tests for pagination edge cases

docs.title: 💙 Thank You!
docs.text:
Every contribution helps make Apaleo API Client better for everyone. Whether you're fixing a typo, adding a feature, or helping with documentation, your efforts are appreciated!

Questions? Don't hesitate to: - Open a discussion - Ask in issues - Reach out to the maintainers

Happy coding! 🚀

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Get started with Apaleo API Client quickly and easily. The library supports Python 3.10+ and can be installed using your preferred package manager.

docs.title: Using pip (recommended)
docs.text: pip install apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Using Poetry
docs.text: poetry add apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Using pipenv
docs.text: pipenv install apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Using conda
docs.text: conda install -c conda-forge apaleo-api-client

docs.title: 🔧 Development Installation
docs.text: For development or contributing to the project:

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/lipppy/apaleo-api-client.git
cd apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Install with Poetry (Recommended)
docs.text:
# Install with all development dependencies
poetry install --with dev,test,lint,docs

# Activate the virtual environment
poetry shell

docs.title: Install with pip
docs.text:
# Create virtual environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate

# Install in development mode
pip install -e ".[dev,test,lint,docs]"

docs.title: Python Version
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • Python 3.13 is fully supported
  • Tested on CPython (PyPy support coming soon)

docs.title: Operating Systems
docs.text:
• ✔️ Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS, Alpine)
  • ✔️ macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon)
  • ✔️ Windows (10, 11, Server)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
Core dependencies (automatically installed):

httpx>=0.28.1        # Async HTTP client
pydantic>=2.12.5     # Data validation and serialization
dacite>=1.9.2        # Dataclass conversion
httpx-retries>=0.4.6 # HTTP retry middleware
polyfactory>=3.3.0   # Testing factories

docs.title: 🗂️ Verify Installation
docs.text:
Test that the installation was successful:

from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient
from apaleoapi import __version__

print(f"Apaleo API Client version: {__version__}")

# Test client initialization (dry-run mode)
client = ApaleoAPIClient(
    client_id="test",
    client_secret="test",
    dry_run=True
)

print("Installation successful!")

docs.title: 🏡 Getting API Credentials
docs.text: Before using the client, you'll need Apaleo API credentials:

docs.title: 1. Register with Apaleo
docs.text:
1. Visit the Apaleo Developer Portal
  2. Create a developer account or sign in
  3. Navigate to "Applications" or "API Keys"

docs.title: 2. Create an Application
docs.text:
1. Click "Create Application"
  2. Enter your application details:
  3. Name: Your application name
  4. Description: Brief description of your integration
  5. Redirect URI: For OAuth flows (if needed)

docs.title: 3. Get Your Credentials
docs.text: After creating the application, you'll receive: - Client ID: Public identifier for your application - Client Secret: Private secret (keep secure!)

docs.title: 4. Configure Scopes
docs.text: Select the appropriate API scopes for your use case: - core:read - Read access to Core API - core:write - Write access to Core API - identity:read - Read access to Identity API - Additional scopes as needed

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Store your credentials securely using environment variables:

# .env file
APALEO_CLIENT_ID=your-client-id-here
APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET=your-client-secret-here
APALEO_TIMEOUT=30
APALEO_MAX_RETRIES=3
APALEO_MAX_CONCURRENT=5

Load them in your application:

import os
from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient

client = ApaleoAPIClient(
    client_id=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_ID"),
    client_secret=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET"),
    timeout=float(os.getenv("APALEO_TIMEOUT", 30)),
    retries=int(os.getenv("APALEO_MAX_RETRIES", 3)),
    max_concurrent=int(os.getenv("APALEO_MAX_CONCURRENT", 5))
)

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Alternatively, use a configuration file:

# apaleo_config.yaml
apaleo:
  client_id: "your-client-id"
  client_secret: "your-client-secret"
  timeout: 30.0
  retries: 3
  max_concurrent: 5
  dry_run: false

logging:
  level: "INFO"
  format: "%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"

import yaml
from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient

with open("apaleo_config.yaml") as f:
    config = yaml.safe_load(f)

client = ApaleoAPIClient(**config["apaleo"])

docs.title: Credential Management
docs.text:
• ⚠️ Never commit credentials to version control
  • Use environment variables or secure secret management
  • Rotate credentials regularly
  • Use different credentials for development/staging/production

docs.title: Network Security
docs.text:
• Always use HTTPS (enforced by default)
  • Consider using a VPN for sensitive environments
  • Monitor API access logs

docs.title: Application Security
docs.text:
• Validate all user inputs before API calls
  • Implement proper error handling
  • Log security events appropriately
  • Follow the principle of least privilege for API scopes

docs.title: 🔥 Quick Start Example
docs.text:
Put it all together with a complete example:

import asyncio
import os
from apaleoapi import ApaleoAPIClient
from apaleoapi.apaleo.core.v1.dataclasses.inventory import PropertyListParams

async def main():
    # Initialize client with environment variables
    client = ApaleoAPIClient(
        client_id=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_ID"),
        client_secret=os.getenv("APALEO_CLIENT_SECRET")
    )

    try:
        # Fetch first 10 properties
        params = PropertyListParams(page_size=10)
        properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params)

        print(f"Found {properties.count} total properties")
        for prop in properties.items:
            print(f"- {prop.name} ({prop.code})")

    finally:
        # Always clean up
        await client.aclose()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Check Current Version
docs.text:
from apaleoapi import __version__
print(__version__)

docs.title: Upgrade to Latest
docs.text: pip install --upgrade apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Upgrade with Poetry
docs.text: poetry update apaleo-api-client

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text:
If you encounter issues while using the Apaleo API Client, here are some common problems and solutions to help you get back on track:

Would you contribute to this project? If you want to help improve this guide or add solutions for common issues, please check our contributing guidelines and submit a pull request!

docs.title: Why use Apaleo API Client?
docs.text: The Apaleo API Client provides a modern, developer-friendly way to integrate with Apaleo's hospitality platform. Here's why you should choose it over direct API calls or other integration methods.

docs.title: Async-First Architecture
docs.text:
• Built on httpx for high-performance concurrent operations
  • Native async/await support for non-blocking I/O
  • Concurrent request batching for pagination
# Fetch multiple pages concurrently
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(
    PropertyListParams(is_concurrently=True, batch_size=100)
)

docs.title: Smart Pagination
docs.text:
• Automatic pagination handling with concurrent fetching
  • Configurable batch sizes for optimal performance
  • Built-in memory management for large datasets

docs.title: Comprehensive Error Handling
docs.text:
• Typed exceptions for all HTTP status codes
  • Detailed error messages with context
  • Graceful handling of API rate limits and transient failures
from apaleoapi.exceptions import ValidationError, NotFoundError

try:
    property = await client.core.v1.inventory.get_property("invalid-id")
except NotFoundError:
    print("Property not found")
except ValidationError as e:
    print(f"Invalid request: {e.message}")

docs.title: Automatic Token Management
docs.text:
• OAuth2 Client Credentials flow with automatic refresh
  • Token caching and expiry tracking
  • Seamless handling of 401 responses

docs.title: Built-in Retries
docs.text:
• Configurable retry logic for transient failures
  • Exponential backoff with jitter
  • Respect for Retry-After headers

docs.title: Complete Type Coverage
docs.text:
• Full type hints for all methods and responses
  • Pydantic models for request/response validation
  • IDE autocompletion and error detection
# Type-safe parameter construction
params = PropertyListParams(
    page_number=1,          # int
    page_size=50,           # int  
    include_archived=True,  # Optional[bool]
)

# Typed response handling
properties: PropertyList = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties(params)
for property: PropertyItem in properties.items:
    print(property.name)  # IDE knows this is a str

Note

Apaleo's models may have field names that conflict with Python built-in function names, variable names, or keywords. Use caution when naming your variables or consider using aliases to avoid conflicts. For example, if a model has a field named property, you can use an alias like from builtins import property as property_alias in your code to prevent shadowing the built-in @property decorator.

docs.title: Rich Data Models
docs.text:
• Structured dataclasses for all domain objects
  • Automatic serialization/deserialization
  • Validation with detailed error messages

docs.title: Dry-Run Mode
docs.text:
Perfect for development and testing:

# No actual API calls made
client = ApaleoAPIClient(..., dry_run=True)
properties = await client.core.v1.inventory.list_properties()
# Returns empty PropertyList for testing

docs.title: Comprehensive Logging
docs.text:
• Detailed request/response logging
  • Configurable log levels
  • Integration with Python's standard logging module
from apaleoapi.logging import setup_logging

logger = setup_logging(level="DEBUG")
# See all HTTP requests, responses, and timing information

docs.title: Testing Support
docs.text:
• Mock-friendly design with dependency injection
  • Async test patterns with pytest-asyncio
  • Factory classes for test data generation

docs.title: Production Deployment
docs.text:
• Configurable timeouts and connection limits
  • Memory-efficient streaming for large responses
  • Monitoring and observability hooks

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Secure credential handling
  • TLS/SSL verification by default
  • No credential logging in production mode

docs.title: Scalability
docs.text:
• Concurrent request limiting to respect API quotas
  • Efficient connection pooling
  • Horizontal scaling support

docs.title: vs. Direct HTTP Calls
docs.text: ✔️ No manual token management ✔️ Built-in error handling and retries ✔️ Type safety and validation ✔️ Pagination automation ✔️ Consistent API interface

docs.title: vs. Generic API Clients
docs.text: ✔️ Domain-specific error types ✔️ Apaleo-optimized request patterns ✔️ Built-in knowledge of API quirks ✔️ Specialized data models ✔️ Hotel industry domain knowledge

docs.title: vs. Custom Wrappers
docs.text: ✔️ Production-tested reliability ✔️ Comprehensive test coverage ✔️ Active maintenance and updates ✔️ Community support and documentation ✔️ Performance optimizations

docs.title: Property Management Systems (PMS)
docs.text:
• Sync property data and availability
  • Manage rates and inventory
  • Handle reservations and guest information

docs.title: Channel Managers
docs.text:
• Distribute inventory across multiple channels
  • Real-time rate and availability updates
  • Central reservation management

docs.title: Revenue Management
docs.text:
• Analyze booking patterns and performance
  • Dynamic pricing optimization
  • Competitive market analysis

docs.title: Business Intelligence
docs.text:
• Extract data for reporting and analytics
  • Monitor KPIs and performance metrics
  • Historical trend analysis

docs.title: Integration Platforms
docs.text:
• Connect Apaleo with other hospitality systems
  • Data synchronization between platforms
  • Workflow automation

Ready to experience the benefits? Install Apaleo API Client →
